Plague  

Description

1: Increase Disease Counter by 9
2: Decrease Hitpoints by 97 per tick. Max: 156 per tick
3: Decrease HP when cast by 144

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 308 Skill: Conjuration
Casting Time: 3 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Disease
Range: 200 Location: Any
Time of Day: Any Deletable: Yes
Dot Stacking: Yes Reflectable: Yes
Focusable: Yes Dispellable: No
Interruptable: Yes Short Buff Box: No
Target Type: Single Spell Type: Detrimental
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You feel feverish.
Cast on other: Soandso sweats and shivers, looking feverish.
Effect Fades: Your fever has broken.

Game Description

Sickens your target with a fever, causing 144 damage initially and between 97 and @2 damage every six seconds for 1.3 mins.

Items with this effect

  • Ring of Ill Touch
  • Spell: Plague

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Plague
ClassLevel
BST65
NEC52
SHM49
Expansion:

Duration:
1.3 mins

  • Sold By (17)
  • NPCs with Ability (20)

I got this from General Jyleel when I turned in an illegible scroll dropped from an orc oracle. Of course I had to work on faction first so I wasn't KOS, but even at dubious he gave me (DE NEC) this level 52 spell.

2 posts

Abysmal Sea
QuoteReply
# Mar 15 2005 at 8:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256867644
10 posts
Also sold by Yiem Rynawu in the Abysmal Sea (23p, 1g, 5s, 2c at 172 charisma).
▲ Page top
Plague
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2004 at 7:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592266559977
View User Forum
8 posts
Hmm.. not sure about all necros, but most solo. Solo means longer fight. At level 55+ would you pass on this DoT when fighting gargs in PoN? I'd hope not since I can run 2 or 3 splurt durations on them. You're going to do a lot of damage over time with this, and frankly it's a good spell.

Cast dooming, splurt, ignite and plague on a mob and refresh dooming as needed, watch it's HP eventually drop hard just like you were using higher damage DoTs, except, you've been able to spend this time medding back to FM for your next kill, or, if RRing, you are already killing next mob with these same DoTs.

In short, it has a lot of uses, but for root rotting, it's awesome because you can run multiple mobs at once due to the nice duration. I could see keeping 3 mobs DoTed at once, not missing a tick, with our 3 long duration DoTs.
▲ Page top
Info
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2004 at 2:01 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788108315
89 posts
Level 34 Scourge = 544 DMG 170 Mana
Level 39 Venom = 453 DMG 160 Mana
Level 49 Envenomed = 1132 DMG 320 Mana
Level 49 Plauge = 1215 DMG 300 Mana

3344 DMG 950 Mana
▲ Page top
Sold in OT
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2004 at 1:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592761805664
11 posts
Also sold by Finlay Kitoran in OT. Cost with 142 charisma was 43p 4g 8s 7c.
▲ Page top
Stop that Regen!
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2004 at 4:40 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592261250934
Scholar
98 posts
As others have said before me. The reason you cast a disease-based DoT isn't so much for the DPS. It's used in situations where it's more important to stop the MOB's natural regen. Cazel was an excellent example.

Throw a disease-based DoT on something and you're stopping it from healing. Not only that, you're also doing a token amount of damage per tick.

Like many of our spells, it's situational.
▲ Page top
RE: Stop that Regen!
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2004 at 8:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775566052
117 posts
This is a myth, unfortunately.

Disease based dots don't stop regen. They merely look like they do because of the hp they're taking off at a slower pace than the poison dots.
▲ Page top
Other vendors..
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2003 at 7:52 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788774732
11 posts
Any other vendors have this spell?
▲ Page top
Plauge
QuoteReply
# Oct 13 2003 at 11:19 PM Rating: Default
Drauger
16 posts

# Sep 21 2004 at 10:43 PM Rating: Decent

Scholar
243 posts

▲ Page top
Why buy it
QuoteReply
# Jun 08 2003 at 9:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592257159340
58 posts
There is one quite good reason to buy this spell. It stacks with the poison based dot. Worth it? Maybe, if you want that extra bit of damage.
▲ Page top
Why?
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 10:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252617621
View User Forum
55 posts
Can anyone explain to me why I would purchase this spell. More manna than other spells, less damage, both initially and over time. Does it last forever? Thats the only reason I could thing of that SOE would make a spell like this for this level.
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2003 at 11:20 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592342878485
Scholar
85 posts
Another good reason to use it is for grabbing aggro back off of those whiny healers when the group gets adds. Poison dot/disease dot/slow are a great way to fix a mob's attention without having to chase all over swinging your blades at him.

Sharrien Dreamstalker
Elder Savage Lord of 63 Seasons
Vazaelle
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# May 14 2003 at 10:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
Disease lasts three times as long as poison based DoTs. They actually do MORE damage overall, and cost less mana per pt damage.

Also, they stop MoB regeneration.
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2003 at 5:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252617621
View User Forum
55 posts
So this spell does 1215 hp dmg for 300 mana over 21 ticks as opposed to something like Envenomed bolt which does 1132 hp dmg for 320 for 7 tickes. Most of my kills do not last more than about a minute and a half depending on where I am hunting.
IMO, this is not that great of a spell.
But like you said, it will knock off any disease based buffs if the mob has that
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2003 at 3:01 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966228402
194 posts
You forget something very simple... The spells stack. So yes, the Poison DOT line is usually better (probably always better, really) because it does much more damage per tick, but when you're fighting a particularly tough mob, the addition of a disease DOT helps.
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2003 at 3:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781751300
21 posts
Disease-based buffs? Doesn't the idea of a disease conferring a beneficial effect sound odd?

The only semi-beneficial disease I can think of is lycanthropy (cheer if you played a shapeshifter in BG2!)
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2003 at 4:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245745802
View User Forum
Sage
950 posts
Well, he mean MoBs with natural regeneration.
▲ Page top
Why?
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 10:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252617621
View User Forum
55 posts
Can anyone explain to me why I would purchase this spell. More manna than other spells, less damage, both initially and over time. Does it last forever? Thats the only reason I could thing of that SOE would make a spell like this for this level.
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2003 at 10:15 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592343631709
118 posts
There is a TIME and a PLACE for a spell like this to be used: on Mobs with tons of HP.

If you are on a raid or in a group, and realistically, it is gonna be a 3 to 5 minute fight (or longer), this is one more dot you can have working FOR YOU, while everyone else is doing their part. It is not a spell for fights that are short. EB would be a candidate for that instead.

So, learn WHEN to use certain spell. Some, like this one, have a good use. Like when you need a little extra dot going, on any long encounter.

▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2003 at 2:37 AM Rating: Default
Phluxor
View User Forum
3 posts
Also, keep in mind....your stoping the regen of the Mob and if the fight lasts 5-10 mins it is doing more damage by stoping regen than it is with it's Dps =)
____________________________
/afk looking up info on Allakhazam's site
/snort
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2003 at 1:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595175496031
101 posts
You brought up a very good point about this spell and other disease based spells. If you ever have to fight Cazel in Oasis, you will LOVE this spell because he will not be able to regen with it on him.. and trust me.. he HAS to have his regen stopped because it is incredibly fast.

I was unable to solo him with my wizard (and yes he does summon but not rapidly), because even though I was nuking the l;jlkj out of him (over 3k dmg a nuke - SCF3) he would regen what I was nuking him for just about. I was quickly running out of mana while he was almost staying even in regen with my nukes... What a BEAST! :-) If a necro would have but cast one plague dot on him, I would have owned him with np.. I think if anyone could solo him, it would be a necro or SK. Fear+ Disease DoT.
▲ Page top
RE: Why?
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2004 at 10:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592254348316
3 posts
does anyone know of any other beastlord spell that can stop a mobs regeneration?

thanks.
